TO PRODUCE CUSTODIAN(S) OF RECORDS AND PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE FOR DEPOSITION AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, AND PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION IS PROPERLY SET FOR HEARING ON OR BEFORE THE 15TH DAY BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE AS REQUIRED BY CCP 2024.020. HOWEVER, PURSUANT TO CCP 2024.020, DISCOVERY IS CLOSED AND THE COURT CANNOT COMPEL DEFENDANT TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS AFTER THE CLOSE OF DISCOVERY WHEN PLAINTIFFS DID NOT MOVE TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS CLOSER TO THE TRIAL DATE OR RE-OPEN DISCOVERY. (SEE PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS (2008) 165 CAL.APP.4TH 1568.) EVEN IF THE COURT COULD COMPEL DISCOVERY TO BE COMPLETED, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION WOULD BE DENIED. DEFENDANT IS A DISSOLVED CORPORATION AND IS UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO PRODUCE A DEPONENT. (MALDONADO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 1390.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. Off Calendar Aug-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Jeanette Tackett, et al.
Devon E. Downs of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / opposing party Cahill Construction Co., Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS JEANETTE TACKETT, JENNIFER HENDRIX, STEVEN TACKETT, BRUCE TACKETT'S MOTION TO COMPEL CAHILL CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. TO PRODUCE CUSTODIAN(S) OF RECORDS AND PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE FOR DEPOSITION AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, AND PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel Cahill Construction Co., Inc. to produce person most knowledgeable and custodian of records for deposition and responsive documents is denied. Plaintiffs' motion is properly set for hearing on or before the 15th day before the trial date as required by C.C.P. 2024.020. However, pursuant to C.C.P. 2024.020, discovery is closed and the court cannot compel defendant to conduct discovery proceedings after the close of discovery when plaintiffs did not move to complete discovery proceedings closer to the trial date or re-open discovery. (See Pelton-Shepherd Industries v. Delta Packaging Products (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568.) Even if the court could compel discovery to be completed, plaintiffs' motion would be denied. Defendant is a dissolved corporation and is under no obligation to produce a deponent. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Jennifer Seelenbacher, CSR #11878 (503/TLJ)
